# ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 - Cite-Only Pointer

This document is a cite-only pointer. It names ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 and the clauses the BRD Builder workflow references, links to the ISO catalog entry, and does not redistribute the standard's text.

## What ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Is

ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018, *Systems and software engineering - Life cycle processes - Requirements engineering*, specifies the requirements-engineering process across the system and software life cycle. It defines the activities of requirements elicitation, analysis, specification, verification, and validation, and prescribes the content and characteristics of stakeholder, system, and software requirements specifications.

The BRD Builder references ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 by clause and section only. The clauses most relevant to the BRD workflow are:

* The §5.2.5 individual-requirement quality characteristics clause - source of the nine characteristics (necessary, appropriate, unambiguous, complete, singular, feasible, verifiable, correct, conforming) referenced in [../SKILL.md#quality-dimensions-and-rubrics](../SKILL.md#quality-dimensions-and-rubrics).
* The stakeholder requirements specification (StRS) clause - source of the stakeholder-need expression posture used during the Discover phase.
* The system requirements specification (SyRS) clause - source of the FR / NFR / constraint partition referenced in [../SKILL.md#requirement-categories](../SKILL.md#requirement-categories).

The BRD Builder does not implement the full ISO process. It adopts the standard's vocabulary and quality criteria, then routes detailed system and software requirements down to the PRD and downstream specifications.

## Why Cite-Only

ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 is a paywalled international standard. Its text is not freely redistributable and is not embedded anywhere in this repository.

The BRD Builder's posture is:

* Reference the standard by its full designator and the relevant clause name when explaining a requirements characteristic, quality attribute, or process activity.
* Do not paraphrase the standard's normative text. Definitions and rubrics in this repository are written from scratch as original Microsoft content informed by, but not derived from, the standard.
* When a stakeholder asks for the canonical text of a clause, link them to the ISO catalog entry below.

## Upstream Source

[https://www.iso.org/standard/72089.html](https://www.iso.org/standard/72089.html) - ISO catalog entry where the current revision, scope, and purchase terms are obtained.
